english: what is secret, hidden, ysaṅ (gsang) śor (shor) the secret comes out, is made known Dzl.

I. vb. to do a thing secretly, to conceal, ṅa-la (nga la) ysaṅ-du (gsang du) mi (mi) ruṅ (rung) it is not right of you to be so close to me Mil.; ysaṅ-ste (gsang ste) brkús-nas (brkus nas) stealing secretly, ysaṅ-ste (gsang ste) bskyál-nas (bskyal nas) sending under-hand, furtively Dzl.; to hide one's self, to be concealed dbén-pai (dben pai) ynas (gnas) śig-tu (shig tu) ysaṅ-ste (gsang ste) betaking one's self secretly to a solitary place Dzl., yid-mtún-par (yid mthun par) ysaṅ-ste (gsang ste) keeping it secret with one consent Dzl.; *saṅ-ṇe (sang ṇe) dad-cè (dad ce)* W. to sit concealed. II. sbst. 1. secret things, a secret; ysaṅ-bai (gsang bai) bdag-po (bdag po) = ysaṅ-dbaṅ (gsang dbang) v. below. — 2. secret parts Med., also ysaṅ-ynás (gsang gnas) Med., ysaṅ-bai (gsang bai) pád-ma (pad ma) Med., sometimes the anus included; ysaṅ-bai (gsang bai) nad (nad) diseases of the sexual organs Med. III. adj. 1. secret, hidden, concealed, ysaṅ-bai (gsang bai) čos (chos) esoteric doctrine Dzl.; pýi-naṅ-ysaṅ (phyi nang gsang) v. pýi (phyi) III. — 2. kyi-mo (kyi mo) nyan-gyi (nyan gyi) rna (rna) ysaṅ-ba (gsang ba) a female dog of very sharp hearing (v. yseṅ-ba (gseng ba)). Comp. ysaṅ-kaṅ (gsang khang) a secret room Cs. — ysaṅ-sgo (gsang sgo) a secret door Pth. — ysaṅ-sgro (gsang sgro) S.g.? — ysaṅ-sṅágs (gsang sngags) secret charms, mysterious incantations, frq.; even in medical works they are praised as the 'best medicine'. — ysaṅ-mje (gsang mje) v. mje (mje). — ysaṅ-ynás (gsang gnas) 1. a secret place. 2. mystery, nif.: ysaṅ-bai (gsang bai) ynas (gnas) dú-ma (du ma) ysuṅs (gsungs) he taught many mysteries, many secret doctrines. 3. privities, pudenda. — ysaṅ-spyód (gsang spyod) privy, necessary, water-closet. — ysaṅ-dbaṅ (gsang dbang), ysaṅ-rdór (gsang rdor) Mil., ysaṅ-bai-bdág-po (gsang bai bdag po) Do. = rdo-rje-čaṅ (rdo rje chang), v. rdo-rje (rdo rje), comp.

resp. to hear, to listen Dzl.; kyod (khyod) ysan (gsan) daṅ (dang) listen (to me)! Mil.; bdag-gi (bdag gi) tsig-la (tshig la) yson (gson) id. Glr.; with las (las) or la (la): to hear a person teaching, expounding etc. Tar. Cf. ysón-pa (gson pa).

, ysár-pa (gsar pa), usually ysár-po (gsar po) new, fresh, lúg-śa (lug sha) ysár-ba (gsar ba) fresh mutton Lt.; rma (rma) ysár-pa (gsar pa) a raw wound Thgy.; bág-ma (bag ma) ysár-pa (gsar pa) the young, (recently married) wife Dzl.; *bhú-mo (bhu mo) sár-pa (sar pa)* a girl that is still a virgin C.; ysár-du (gsar du) adv., ysár-du (gsar du) bsád-pai (bsad pai) śa (sha) flesh of animals that have just been slaughtered, lit.: fresh-slaughtered flesh Dzl.; ka-ysár (kha gsar) a new edge; kaṅ-ysár (khang gsar) new house, also a name of villages, castles etc.; gos-ysár (gos gsar), mar-ysár (mar gsar) Lt.; rta-ysár (rta gsar) Schr. a horse not yet broken in. — ysar (gsar) grógs-pa (grogs pa) Sch.: 'to tell each other news; to make a new acquaintance'. *sar-zúg (sar zug) cò-cè (co ce)* W. to plant (a piece of land) for the first time, to cultivate, to people, to stock with inhabitants. — ysar-riṅ (gsar rnying) old and new, stale and fresh; age, duration, existence. — ysar (gsar) yčód-pa (gcod pa) frq., also tsar (tshar) yčód-pa (gcod pa) Pth., to search, inquire into, investigate thoroughly, to examine, to study, skad (skad) a language. — ysár-bu (gsar bu) new beginner, tyro, novice Mil.

vb., to be clear, distinct, bright, slar (slar) śár-źiṅ (shar zhing) ysál-na (gsal na) when (the sun) shines bright again; már-me (mar me) ₒči-kar ('chi khar) ysál-ba (gsal ba) bźin (bzhin) flaming up once more, like an extinguishing lamp Glr.; lhaṅ-ṅé (lhang nge) lham-mér (lham mer) ysál-te (gsal te) appearing bright, clear and distinct Dzl.; ysál-lo (gsal lo) it is clear, it is evident; it stands written, it may be read, ₒdúl-ba-na ('dul ba na) ysál-lo (gsal lo) it may be read in the Dulva Glr., Tar.; ysál-po (gsal po) (प्रकाश्) visible to a great distance, conspicuous, distinct, obvious, intelligible; kun-ysál (kun gsal) id.; *mig (mig) sál-po (sal po) toṅ (tong) mi (mi) tub (thub)*, *ṅag (ngag) sál-po (sal po) zer (zer) mi (mi) śe (she)* W. his eye, -- his speech, is not clear, he is not able to see, to speak distinctly; ysal-dag-snyan-ysúm (gsal dag snyan gsum) B. = the popular sgra-dag-ysal-ysúm (sgra dag gsal gsum), v. sgra (sgra); yi-ge (yi ge) ysál-po (gsal po) a plain, legible handwriting; clear, bright, ysál-bai (gsal bai) mé-loṅ (me long) a bright mirror (a frq. title of books); bright, light, pure, of colours, dkar-ysál (dkar gsal) pure white Glr.; pure, free from faults and deficiencies, *sál-po (sal po) gyáb-cè (gyab ce)* W. to correct; sa-pýógs (sa phyogs) kyaṅ (kyang) ysál-bar (gsal bar) gyúr-ro (gyur ro) also his whole neighbourhood will be freed from defects, will become happy Do.; ysál-le-ba (gsal le ba) = ysal-ba (gsal ba); ysál-ka (gsal kha) Tar. prob. = ysál-ča (gsal cha), Mil.: ysál-ča (gsal cha) źig (zhig) gnán-ba (gnan ba) źu (zhu) I request (you) to give me a detailed account, inst. of which also only sal (sal) čig (chig) may be said.
